destinations.evidence: Settlement reads skip missing jobs and continue building a result map for existing jobs.

equivalence_argument: The settlement reader treats zero jobExecutor as an omitted result, not a fatal database error, so builder/verifier callers receive existing settlement jobs and decide how to handle absences.
notes: This is the current settlement-reader behavior and is intentionally different from SPC fail-closed reads.
rationale: The primary diff changes missing jobExecutor handling in read_jobs_for_settlement from an immediate DatabaseError to continue, and the commit message explains builder callers need graceful skip behavior. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: read_jobs_for_settlement() must skip job IDs whose AsyncJobTracker jobExecutor slot is zero instead of returning an error, leaving builder/verifier callers to decide how to handle absent settlement jobs.
suggested_falsification_tests: Call read_jobs_for_settlement with a mix of existing and nonexistent job IDs and assert the nonexistent IDs are omitted from the returned map without error, while existing jobs are still decoded.
summary: Settlement reads skip missing jobs without error.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Call read_jobs_for_settlement with a mix of existing and nonexistent job IDs and assert the nonexistent IDs are omitted from the returned map without error, while existing jobs are still decoded.
title: Settlement reads skip missing jobs
